title: Li Auto Earnings Focus as Huawei Competition Intensifies
text: Li Auto's earnings are increasingly in focus as Huawei-backed AITO competition intensifies in the Chinese premium EV market. The AITO M9 has successfully captured market share from Li Auto's flagship L9 model through superior integration of Huawei's ecosystem, including seamless connectivity and software features. Investors are watching how Li Auto manages pricing and profitability amid aggressive competitive pressures. The company reported Q3 2024 revenue of RMB 42.9 billion, but faced margin compression challenges as competitive intensity forced price adjustments. Management's ability to maintain premium positioning while defending market share will be critical for earnings sustainability. Recent earnings announcements have shown margin deterioration, with vehicle-specific gross margin declining from above 20.9% in Q3 2024 to lower levels as the year progressed. The introduction of new models including the Li i8 and upcoming i6 BEVs adds complexity to margin management as the company transitions from its traditional EREV strength toward pure electric vehicles. Analysts are focused on whether Li Auto can achieve profitability targets while maintaining R&D investments needed to compete with Huawei's rapid innovation cycle.
